mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of 2018-06-15 reflects recent Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days. Signal activates only when monthly payroll growth exceeds the threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148,912 thousand as of 2018-06-15 indicates near-threshold employment level
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses profit margins and equity valuations. The mechanism requires simultaneous weakness in both series to activate, indicating broad-based demand contraction rather than sector-specific weakness.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 3% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501,971 million and HOUST at 1,350 thousand as of 2018-05-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST declines, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, which constrains earnings growth and produces negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO crossings below 3-month MA are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% as of 2018-06-15 remains below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations. The interaction of elevated inflation and restrictive rates produces negative returns as the Fed maintains hawkish stance.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.857 and FEDFUNDS at 1.82% as of 2018-06-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. Signal activates only when growth exceeds the threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,371.854 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2018-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth and construction sector earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,350 thousand units as of 2018-05-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances following HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
